description:
You are the site administrator and have been asked to identify every user who has successfully logged in at least once, using the system log that is already present on the machine.

1. The log you must analyse is located at  
   /home/user/logs/user_actions.log

   Every line in that file follows this exact format (four space-separated fields, no additional whitespace):

   YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS USERNAME ACTION

   Example line (do **not** create this line yourself):  
   2024-05-18 14:02:07 alice LOGIN

   • YYYY-MM-DD is the date.  
   • HH:MM:SS is the time (24-hour clock).  
   • USERNAME is a single lowercase word.  
   • ACTION is one of the words LOGIN, LOGOUT, or FAILED.

2. Task to perform  
   Produce a plain-text report containing the list of all distinct USERNAMEs that appear in a line whose ACTION exactly equals “LOGIN”. The list must:

   • be sorted in strict alphabetical order (a–z)  
   • show one USERNAME per line  
   • contain no duplicates  
   • end with a single trailing newline and **no** extra blank lines

3. Save this report to the file  
   /home/user/reports/logged_in_users.txt  
   (Create the /home/user/reports directory if it does not already exist.)

4. Accomplish everything with a single command line (you can use pipes and redirections, but chain them in one line).

5. When you are done, you may display the contents of /home/user/reports/logged_in_users.txt so the output can be visually inspected, but ensure the file itself is created exactly as specified above—the automated tests will read that file directly.

Reminder: do not modify /home/user/logs/user_actions.log; only read from it and write the required output file.
truth:
Initial setup before the agent starts:

1. The directory /home/user/logs already exists (0755).  
2. The file /home/user/logs/user_actions.log exists with the following exact content (10 lines, each terminated by a single LF):

2024-05-18 08:14:22 alice LOGIN
2024-05-18 08:16:30 bob LOGIN
2024-05-18 08:20:44 alice LOGOUT
2024-05-18 09:01:07 charlie FAILED
2024-05-18 09:02:12 diana LOGIN
2024-05-18 09:05:55 charlie LOGIN
2024-05-18 09:07:01 charlie LOGOUT
2024-05-18 09:10:33 bob LOGOUT
2024-05-18 09:11:11 edward LOGIN
2024-05-18 09:15:00 frank FAILED

3. The directory /home/user/reports does NOT exist at the start of the task.

Expected result after successful task completion:

1. Directory /home/user/reports is created (0755).  
2. File /home/user/reports/logged_in_users.txt exists, mode 0644, containing exactly the following five lines (alphabetically sorted usernames, each followed by LF, with no blank lines before, after, or between):

alice
bob
charlie
diana
edward

(There is a single LF after “edward”.)

Automated tests will:

• Check that /home/user/reports/logged_in_users.txt exists.  
• Verify that its contents are exactly the five lines shown above, including the trailing newline, and nothing more.  
• Optionally, confirm that only one command was entered on the agent’s final execution line (pipes are allowed).
